hero.title: Track your behavior.
hero.subTitle: Improve your focus.
hero.description: LazyNevis is a privacy-first desktop focus tool. It watches the active window during a session, classifies your time as focused or distracted, and nudges you when you drift — without ever blocking an app or sending data anywhere.
hero.tagline: LazyNevis - for lazy people who don't give upis.
hero.iconAlt: LazyNevis app icon — red bucket hat mascot

about.title: Observe. Classify. Nudge.
about.description: Most focus apps block distractions or lock you into timers. LazyNevis takes a different path.
about.p1: During a session, LazyNevis polls the active window every second and classifies that second as <focused>focused</focused>, <distracted>distracted</distracted>, or <idle>idle</idle> according to rules you define.
about.p2: If distraction accumulates past a threshold you set, it fires an alert — a native notification, a fullscreen overlay, or a sound. You decide what to do next. <bold>Nothing is blocked.</bold>
about.p3: Visible but not intrusive: the tray icon keeps LazyNevis out of your way while global shortcuts let you control sessions without switching windows.

localFirst.title: Local-first
localFirst.description: All session data, settings, and audio files live in your OS application-data directory. No cloud sync.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: No accounts, analytics, or telemetry. The only outbound request is a manual "Check for updates" from GitHub.

noTelemetry.title: No telemetry
noTelemetry.description: Accessibility permission on your OS reads window titles only — never keystrokes. Your focus patterns stay on your machine.

features.title: Everything you need to stay aware
features.description: Session tools, classification, alerts, and history — all running locally on your machine.

session.title: Session management
session.description: Start, pause, resume, and stop with crash-safe recovery. Live timers and manual checkpoints.

classification.title: Focus classification
classification.description: Allowlist or blocklist rules by app name or browser tab title. LazyNevis never flags itself.

alerts.title: Alerts & notifications
alerts.description: Native notifications, fullscreen overlay, or sound — with configurable thresholds and cooldowns.

audio.title: Audio
audio.description: Built-in sounds plus custom MP3, WAV, or OGG files with looping overlay audio.

breaks.title: Breaks
breaks.description: Configurable break intervals based on focused time, with countdown and per-session history.

history.title: History & export
history.description: Visual timelines, charts, and export to JSON or CSV for any date range.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: English and Portuguese, light/dark themes, global shortcuts, and optional autostart.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: No accounts, cloud sync, analytics, or telemetry. Manual update check only.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: From session start to history review — a transparent loop you control.

1.title: Start a session
1.description: Begin from the Dashboard or with a global shortcut whenever you are ready to focus.

2.title: Monitor active window
2.description: The monitor polls the active window every second using native OS APIs on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

3.title: Classify each second
3.description: The rule engine marks time as focused, distracted, or idle based on your allowlist or blocklist rules.

4.title: Timers accumulate
4.description: Focus, distraction, and idle totals update in real time on the Dashboard.

5.title: Alerts when needed
5.description: When distraction crosses your threshold, notifications, overlay, or sound fire — nothing is blocked.

6.title: Review history
6.description: Everything saves to local SQLite. Review timelines, charts, and exports on the History page.

install.title: Download & install
install.description: Install via the official script — it downloads the latest release, verifies the SHA-256 checksum, and sets up LazyNevis automatically.
install.detectedHint: We detected your system — showing the recommended tab.

requirements.macos: macOS 12+ · Apple Silicon (ARM64). Intel Macs are not currently supported.
requirements.windows: Windows 10+ · x64 or ARM64. Open PowerShell or PowerShell 7 (not Command Prompt).
requirements.linux: x64 (amd64) · WebKit2GTK and a system tray library required. ARM64 is not supported on Linux.

install.prereleaseNote: Pre-releases are skipped unless you add the prerelease flag explicitly.
install.releasesError: Could not load release info. You can still use the commands below or visit GitHub Releases.
install.noReleases: No installable releases are available yet. Check GitHub Releases for updates.
install.linuxDepsCmd: sudo apt-get install libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libgtk-3-0 libayatana-appindicator3-1
install.warningBody: Release candidates may be unsigned while signing credentials are being established. macOS users may need a one-time Gatekeeper bypass; Windows users may see SmartScreen when the installer runs.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Common questions about privacy, platforms, and how LazyNevis works.

block.q: Does LazyNevis block websites or apps?
block.a: No. LazyNevis watches, classifies, and nudges — it never blocks an app or website. You always decide what to do when an alert fires.

cloud.q: Is my data sent to the cloud?
cloud.a: No. All session data, settings, and audio files stay in your OS application-data directory. There is no cloud sync, account, analytics, or telemetry.

classification.a: During a session, LazyNevis polls the active window every second. A rule engine classifies each second as focused, distracted, or idle using your allowlist or blocklist rules. Browser tab titles can be matched separately from the host app.

platforms.a: macOS 12+ (Apple Silicon ARM64), Windows 10+ (x64 and ARM64), and Linux x64 (AppImage, DEB, RPM). Download only from official GitHub Releases.

telemetry.a: No telemetry, analytics, or background update checks. The only network request is an explicit "Check for updates" button that reads public release metadata from GitHub.

macosPerms.a: Accessibility (required) to read the active app name and window title — not keystrokes. Notifications are optional and only needed for native notification alerts.

crash.q: What happens if the app crashes mid-session?
crash.a: Sessions are snapshotted every five seconds to local SQLite. On next launch, the session is recovered in a paused state so no time is lost.

license.a: LazyNevis is MIT licensed. You are free to use, modify, and distribute it according to the license terms.

signed.a: RC builds may be unsigned while signing credentials are being established. See the Gatekeeper (macOS) and SmartScreen (Windows) troubleshooting guides for one-time bypass steps.

footer.tagline: Privacy-first focus for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
